{"credits": [{"type": "analyst", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/159286161?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/vnth4nhnt", "id": 159286161, "login": "vnth4nhnt", "node_id": "U_kgDOCX6DkQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnth4nhnt", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:N/SC:H/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "### Impact

  The default `security.http.urls` policy denies requests to loopback, internal,
  and cloud-metadata IPv4 literals (e.g. `http://127.0.0.1/`,
  `http://169.254.169.254/`). The deny rule only matched dotted-decimal notation,
  so alternate IPv4 encodings of the same addresses — integer, hex, or octal,
  which contain no dot — passed the policy:

  - `http://2130706433/` → `127.0.0.1`
  - `http://2852039166/` → `169.254.169.254` (cloud metadata)
  - `http://0x7f000001/`, `http://017700000001/`, `http://0/`

  When a template passes an untrusted or data-derived URL to
  `resources.GetRemote` and the host platform uses the
  cgo system resolver, these encodings resolve to the blocked address — allowing
  build-time server-side requests to loopback and internal services, including the
  cloud-metadata endpoint in hosted/CI builds. The same check is reused on
  redirects, so the gap also applies to each redirect hop.

  This affects sites that rely on `security.http.urls` as a security boundary
  while fetching attacker-influenced remote URLs; it does not affect sites that
  fully trust the URLs they fetch.

  ### Patches

  Fixed in **v0.163.1**. Integer/hex/octal IPv4 hosts are now canonicalized to
  dotted-decimal before the policy is applied, so every encoding of an address is
  treated alike. No configuration change is required.

  ### Workarounds

  Avoid passing untrusted URLs to `resources.GetRemote`, or
  tighten `security.http.urls` to an explicit allow-list of trusted hosts.

  ### Affected versions

  v0.162.0 – v0.163.0 (patched in v0.163.1).", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-19T19:18:14Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-19T19:18:14Z", "references": ["https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/security/advisories/GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/gohugoio/hugo/security-advisories/GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo", "summary": "Hugo: security.http.urls deny rules bypassed by alternate IPv4 encodings (SSRF)", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-19T19:18:16Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-r46f-3rpw-hxrv", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.163.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/gohugoio/hugo"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.162.0, < 0.163.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Hugo: security.http.urls deny rules bypassed by alternate IPv4 encodings (SSRF)
